metadata.title:
SUFFRAGETTES, DO GOOD


WORK A TTI1E BIG FAIR

pages.raw_text:
Editor The Press-In one or two
issues you have referred to the suf-
fragists. saying they had to borrow
babies Every baby that was in
the suffrage babies auto in the pa
rade woman's day was the child of
fathers and mothers who believe in
equal suffrage. In your article yes
terday with regard to taking the or
phans at the Children's home to the
fair was a good one, with the ex-
ception of the paragraph stating the
suffragists who had to borrow
babies" should do this. Now, the
suffragists are pretty busy women
these days working to get the bal
lot for mothers, that they may have
a voice in making laws in protect
ing their children. The club women
of this city, especially the Woman s
club, have established day nurs-
cry, of which the average number
of babies taken care of daily is 35,
and are now maintaining a day
nursery at their fair grounds with
an average of about 20 taken care
of daily. and l would suggest that
